# Research - 2026-06-26

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins)

> 1-day delta on 2026-06-25. Three NEW items from 06-25/06-26 upstream
> commits. #1 (opencode #33977) splits the V2 MCP timeout into
> `startup` vs `request`; our single `McpServerConfig.timeout` at
> `src/mcp/config.ts:31` conflates handshake and per-call. #2 (cline
> #11815) adds shared non-interactive-command wording (`--no-pager`,
> `GIT_PAGER=cat`, `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive`, `--yes`); our
> `bash.ts:83` is a one-liner. #3 (opencode #33392) threads optional
> `strict?: boolean` through tool defs for OpenAI Responses / Codex
> parity; `defineTool` has no `strict` field, so MCP tools with loose
> schemas break gpt-5.5 / gpt-nano routes. cline 06-26 batch (11868,
> 11867, 11860) was VSCode UI; 11861 is upstream-Commander-only.
> kilocode 06-25 batch was VSCode + JetBrains UX. aider 30-day silent.
> claude-code 7/9 commits CHANGELOG. auto-implement queue EMPTY.

## Executive Summary

- **opencode #33977 -> Item #1 (~4h)**: `src/mcp/client.ts:295` uses
  the same `timeout` for `client.connect` (startup) and `:495` for
  per-tool `AbortController`. Filesystem MCP servers take 5-15s to
  spawn but answer in <500ms; one number can't serve both.
- **cline #11815 -> Item #2 (~2h)**: Pure prompt change in `bash.ts`
  + `code.txt`. Hung agent runs from `git log`, `apt install`,
  `npm init` are the second-largest stuck-turn source after
  permission denials.
- **opencode #33392 -> Item #3 (~3h)**: SAP-routed `gpt-5.5`,
  `gpt-nano`, `claude-haiku` use OpenAI Responses semantics requiring
  `additionalProperties: false`. MCP tools violate this; Codex CLI
  hardcodes `strict: false` to compensate.
- **Carryover**: #772 demote-claude-code still pending after 6 briefs.
  No open `auto-implement` issues -- engineering has bandwidth.

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. Split MCP timeout into startup vs request budgets (Category: mcp, ~4h)

- **What**: Extend `McpServerConfig.timeout` to accept a number
  (legacy: both phases) or `{ startup?: number; request?: number }`
  (defaults 30000 / 60000). In `src/mcp/client.ts`, use `startup` at
  `:295` (`client.connect`) and `request` at `:495` (tool-call
  `AbortController`). Replace `getTimeoutForServer` with
  `getTimeoutsForServer` returning both. Tests: legacy `5000` -> both
  phases; object form -> per-phase; partial -> default fallback.
- **Why**: Today users pick one number; high startup blocks per-call
  latency, low startup kills `connectFromConfig` cold spawns. Split
  lets autoConnect succeed while keeping tool calls tight.
- **Source**: sst/opencode#33977 (commit `f428755`, 2026-06-26,
  `+41 -20`, `packages/core/src/config/mcp.ts`).
- **Category**: mcp. **Size**: small (~4h).

#### 2. Expand bash tool's non-interactive command guidance (Category: tools, ~2h)

- **What**: Replace the single line at `src/tool/tools/bash.ts:83`
  with a 4-5 line block: (a) `--yes`/`-y`/`--non-interactive`/
  `--assume-yes`; (b) disable pagers via `git --no-pager`,
  `GIT_PAGER=cat`, `PAGER=cat`, `| cat`; (c) `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive`
  for `apt`, `CI=true` for JS CLIs; (d) never invoke editors (`vi`,
  `nano`, `less`); (e) `-o BatchMode=yes` / `sudo -n`. Mirror into
  `src/agent/prompts/code.txt:42` (replacing the isolated `--no-pager`
  line) and `default.txt` if absent. No runtime code change.
- **Why**: The agent regularly issues `git log` (pager), `apt install`
  (debconf), or `npm init` (wizard), freezing the bash tool until
  timeout. Prompt-only fix, zero rollout risk.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11815 (commit `bebc581`, 2026-06-25, `+8 -4`,
  `sdk/packages/core/src/extensions/tools/definitions.ts`).
- **Category**: tools. **Size**: small (~2h).

#### 3. Thread optional `strict` flag through tool definitions (Category: tools, ~3h)

- **What**: Add `strict?: boolean` to `ToolDefinition` in
  `src/tool/index.ts:67` and the payload emitted by `zodToJsonSchema`
  at `:343`. `defineTool` accepts `strict`, default `undefined`
  (omitted). In `src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts:950` (`setTools`),
  forward into `{ type: 'function', function: { ..., strict } }`. For
  MCP-bridged tools (`src/mcp/client.ts` registration), default
  `strict: false` so loose remote schemas register. Tests: built-in
  without `strict` -> omits; with `strict: true` -> emits true;
  MCP-bridged -> emits false.
- **Why**: SAP-routed `gpt-5.5`, `gpt-nano`, `claude-haiku` use OpenAI
  Responses API requiring `additionalProperties: false` and no unions.
  MCP tools violate both; today they silently drop or error. Mirrors
  Codex CLI's workaround with no SAP-side change.
- **Source**: sst/opencode#33392 (commit `5f61d21`, 2026-06-25,
  `+31 -9`, `packages/llm/src/protocols/openai-responses.ts`,
  `packages/opencode/src/session/llm/request.ts`).
- **Category**: tools. **Size**: small (~3h).

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Order**: #2 (2h, prompt-only) -> #3 (3h, tool surface) -> #1 (4h,
   MCP config). #2 first (risk-free); #3 before #1 since both touch
   MCP and #3 stabilizes tool registration before timeout reshape.
2. **Reliability lever**: #2 = highest hang-rate ROI; #3 unblocks
   small-model deployments silently dropping MCP tools.
3. **Pipeline health**: auto-implement queue EMPTY. Engineering can
   absorb all three this week.
